steps.title: Identify the structures on each side of the blank
steps.content:
The left side is an independent clause awaiting completion: “Bear River High School and Bryce Valley High School are two of several Utah...” followed by space for a noun phrase with a potential modifier.

The right side, starting with “enormous geoglyph,” is an independent clause: “[An] enormous geoglyph of the letters BR overlooks Bear River High, while...

steps.title: Apply the rules for restrictive clauses and sentence boundaries
steps.content:
Choice B uses the restrictive relative clause “that have their own hillside geoglyphs” without a comma before “that,” and separates the two independent clauses with a period.

Choices A and C incorrectly place a comma before “that,” while choice D uses only a comma to join two independent clauses (a comma splice).

That matches choice B — B is correct.
